advantages: Compresses 'how do I start?' into 'fill in the template'.
Surfaces the right data structure and complexity bound early.
Makes solutions readable to other engineers ('that's a sliding window').
Transfers directly across domains — the same patterns underlie web, ML, infra.
disadvantages: Pattern-matching can blind you to a simpler bespoke solution.
Some problems blend two patterns; misclassifying leads to over-engineering.
Patterns are necessary but not sufficient — implementation details still bite (off-by-one, recursion depth, edge cases).
Mis-recognizing 'optimization' as DP when greedy or sort+sweep would work wastes time.
